@graph.name: Pip identifies himself by a shortened name
@graph.source_quote: So, I called myself Pip, and came to be called Pip.

@graph.name: Pip reflects on his family name and Christian name
@graph.source_quote: My father’s family name being Pirrip, and my Christian name Philip, my infant tongue could make of both names nothing longer or more explicit than Pip.

@graph.name: Identify family name
@graph.source_quote: I give Pirrip as my father’s family name, on the authority of his tombstone and my sister,—Mrs. Joe Gargery, who married the blacksmith.

@graph.name: Acknowledge absence of parents
@graph.source_quote: As I never saw my father or my mother, and never saw any likeness of either of them (for their days were long before the days of photographs)

@graph.name: Formulate first fancies about parents
@graph.source_quote: my first fancies regarding what they were like were unreasonably derived from their tombstones.

@graph.name: Perceive shape of letters on father's tombstone
@graph.source_quote: The shape of the letters on my father’s, gave me an odd idea that he was a square, stout, dark man, with curly black hair.

@graph.name: Pip draws a conclusion about his mother
@graph.source_quote: I drew a childish conclusion that my mother was freckled and sickly.

@graph.name: Pip perceives the inscription
@graph.source_quote: From the character and turn of the inscription, “_Also Georgiana Wife of the Above_,”

@graph.name: Pip perceives the stone lozenges
@graph.source_quote: To five little stone lozenges, each about a foot and a half long, which were arranged in a neat row beside their grave...

@graph.name: Pip reflects on his brothers' early death
@graph.source_quote: ...who gave up trying to get a living, exceedingly early in that universal struggle...

@graph.name: Pip holds a belief about his brothers
@graph.source_quote: I am indebted for a belief I religiously entertained that they had all been born on their backs with their hands in their trousers-pockets...

@graph.name: Pip describes his brothers' state of existence
@graph.source_quote: ...and had never taken them out in this state of existence.

@graph.name: Pip perceives the marsh country
@graph.source_quote: Ours was the marsh country, down by the river, within, as the river wound, twenty miles of the sea.

@graph.name: Pip reflects on his impression
@graph.source_quote: My first most vivid and broad impression of the identity of things seems to me to have been gained on a memorable raw afternoon towards evening.

@graph.name: Pip recognizes the churchyard
@graph.source_quote: I found out for certain that this bleak place overgrown with nettles was the churchyard

@graph.name: Pip learns about the deceased
@graph.source_quote: that Philip Pirrip, late of this parish, and also Georgiana wife of the above, were dead and buried

@graph.name: Pip learns about the infant children
@graph.source_quote: that Alexander, Bartholomew, Abraham, Tobias, and Roger, infant children of the aforesaid, were also dead and buried

@graph.name: Pip perceives the marshes
@graph.source_quote: the dark flat wilderness beyond the churchyard, intersected with dikes and mounds and gates, with scattered cattle feeding on it, was the marshes

@graph.name: Pip perceives the river
@graph.source_quote: the low leaden line beyond was the river

@graph.name: Pip perceives the sea
@graph.source_quote: the distant savage lair from which the wind was rushing was the sea

@graph.name: Pip feels afraid
@graph.source_quote: the small bundle of shivers growing afraid of it all

@graph.name: Pip begins to cry
@graph.source_quote: beginning to cry

@graph.name: A man starts up from among the graves
@graph.source_quote: as a man started up from among the graves at the side of the church porch.

@graph.name: A man cries out to keep quiet
@graph.source_quote: “Hold your noise!” cried a terrible voice,

@graph.name: A man threatens to cut a throat
@graph.source_quote: or I’ll cut your throat!

@graph.name: A man addresses a little devil
@graph.source_quote: Keep still, you little devil,

@graph.name: Man appears
@graph.source_quote: A fearful man, all in coarse grey, with a great iron on his leg.

@graph.name: Man is described
@graph.source_quote: A man with no hat, and with broken shoes, and with an old rag tied round his head.

@graph.name: Man's condition detailed
@graph.source_quote: A man who had been soaked in water, and smothered in mud, and lamed by stones, and cut by flints, and stung by nettles, and torn by briars.

@graph.name: Man exhibits physical symptoms
@graph.source_quote: who limped, and shivered, and glared, and growled; and whose teeth chattered in his head.

@graph.name: Man seizes Pip
@graph.source_quote: as he seized me by the chin.

@graph.name: Pip pleads for his life
@graph.source_quote: Oh! Don’t cut my throat, sir,” I pleaded in terror.

@graph.name: Pip expresses terror
@graph.source_quote: I pleaded in terror.

@graph.name: Pip prays for mercy
@graph.source_quote: Pray don’t do it, sir.

@graph.name: Man stares at Pip
@graph.source_quote: Once more,” said the man, staring at me.

@graph.name: Man commands Pip to give it mouth
@graph.source_quote: Give it mouth!

@graph.name: Man requests location
@graph.source_quote: Show us where you live,” said the man.

@graph.name: Man demands specific location
@graph.source_quote: Pint out the place!

@graph.name: Pip points to the village
@graph.source_quote: I pointed to where our village lay

@graph.name: Pip describes the location of the village
@graph.source_quote: where our village lay, on the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards
@graph.location: the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

@graph.name: Pip indicates the distance to the church
@graph.source_quote: a mile or more from the church
@graph.location: the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

@graph.name: Man looks at Pip
@graph.source_quote: The man, after looking at me for a moment,
@graph.location: the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

@graph.name: Man turns Pip upside down
@graph.source_quote: turned me upside down,
@graph.location: the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

@graph.name: Man empties Pip's pockets
@graph.source_quote: and emptied my pockets.
@graph.location: the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

@graph.name: Pip finds nothing in pockets
@graph.source_quote: There was nothing in them but a piece of bread.
@graph.location: the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards

@graph.name: Church comes to itself
@graph.source_quote: When the church came to itself,

@graph.name: Pip sees the steeple under his feet
@graph.source_quote: I saw the steeple under my feet,

@graph.name: Pip is seated on a high tombstone
@graph.source_quote: I was seated on a high tombstone,

@graph.name: Pip trembles
@graph.source_quote: trembling while he ate the bread ravenously.

@graph.name: Man eats the bread ravenously
@graph.source_quote: while he ate the bread ravenously.

@graph.name: Man comments on Pip's appearance
@graph.source_quote: You young dog,” said the man, licking his lips, “what fat cheeks you ha’ got.

@graph.name: Pip perceives the size of others
@graph.source_quote: I believe they were fat

@graph.name: Pip reflects on his own size and strength
@graph.source_quote: though I was at that time undersized for my years, and not strong

@graph.name: Man expresses desire to eat
@graph.source_quote: Darn me if I couldn’t eat ’em,

@graph.name: Man shakes head threateningly
@graph.source_quote: with a threatening shake of his head,

@graph.name: Man contemplates eating
@graph.source_quote: and if I han’t half a mind to’t!

@graph.name: Pip expresses hope
@graph.source_quote: I earnestly expressed my hope that he wouldn’t

@graph.name: Pip holds tighter to the tombstone
@graph.source_quote: held tighter to the tombstone on which he had put me

@graph.name: Pip tries to avoid crying
@graph.source_quote: partly, to keep myself from crying

@graph.name: Man asks about Pip's mother
@graph.source_quote: “Now lookee here!” said the man. “Where’s your mother?”

@graph.name: Pip starts running
@graph.source_quote: He started, made a short run

@graph.name: Pip stops running
@graph.source_quote: and stopped

@graph.name: Pip looks over his shoulder
@graph.source_quote: and looked over his shoulder

@graph.name: Pip explains his relationship to Georgiana
@graph.source_quote: There, sir!” I timidly explained.

@graph.name: Pip identifies Georgiana as his mother
@graph.source_quote: Also Georgiana. That’s my mother.

@graph.name: Pip expresses surprise
@graph.source_quote: Oh!

@graph.name: Pip asks about family
@graph.source_quote: And is that your father alonger your mother?

@graph.name: Pip returns
@graph.source_quote: said he, coming back.

@graph.name: Pip acknowledges the presence of another person
@graph.source_quote: Yes, sir,” said I; “him too; late of this parish.

@graph.name: Pip is questioned about his living situation
@graph.source_quote: “Who d’ye live with,—supposin’ you’re kindly let to live, which I han’t made up my mind about?”

@graph.name: Magwitch considers his decision about Pip's fate
@graph.source_quote: he muttered then, considering.

@graph.name: Introduction of Mrs. Joe Gargery
@graph.source_quote: My sister, sir,—Mrs. Joe Gargery,—wife of Joe Gargery, the blacksmith, sir.

@graph.name: Magwitch speaks to Pip
@graph.source_quote: “Blacksmith, eh?” said he.

@graph.name: Magwitch looks at his leg
@graph.source_quote: And looked down at his leg.

@graph.name: Magwitch looks at Pip's leg and him
@graph.source_quote: After darkly looking at his leg and me several times

@graph.name: Magwitch approaches Pip's tombstone
@graph.source_quote: he came closer to my tombstone

@graph.name: Magwitch takes Pip by both arms
@graph.source_quote: took me by both arms

@graph.name: Magwitch tilts Pip back
@graph.source_quote: tilted me back as far as he could hold me

@graph.name: Magwitch looks powerfully into Pip's eyes
@graph.source_quote: his eyes looked most powerfully down into mine

@graph.name: Pip looks helplessly up into Magwitch's eyes
@graph.source_quote: and mine looked most helplessly up into his

@graph.name: Magwitch questions Pip about living
@graph.source_quote: Now lookee here,” he said, “the question being whether you’re to be let to live.

@graph.name: Magwitch asks about a file
@graph.source_quote: You know what a file is?

@graph.name: Pip feels helplessness
@graph.source_quote: so as to give me a greater sense of helplessness

@graph.name: Pip perceives danger
@graph.source_quote: so as to give me a greater sense of helplessness and danger

@graph.name: Magwitch tilts Pip
@graph.source_quote: After each question he tilted me over a little more

@graph.name: Request for a file
@graph.source_quote: You get me a file.

@graph.name: Request for wittles
@graph.source_quote: And you get me wittles.

@graph.name: Threat of violence
@graph.source_quote: Or I’ll have your heart and liver out.

@graph.name: Repeated tilting
@graph.source_quote: He tilted me again.

@graph.name: Pip feels frightened
@graph.source_quote: I was dreadfully frightened

@graph.name: Pip feels giddy
@graph.source_quote: and so giddy

@graph.name: Pip clings to Magwitch
@graph.source_quote: I clung to him with both hands

@graph.name: Pip requests assistance from Magwitch
@graph.source_quote: and said, 'If you would kindly please to let me keep upright, sir'

@graph.name: Pip expresses hope to not be sick
@graph.source_quote: perhaps I shouldn’t be sick

@graph.name: Pip expresses hope to attend more
@graph.source_quote: and perhaps I could attend more

@graph.name: Pip experiences a tremendous dip and roll
@graph.source_quote: He gave me a most tremendous dip and roll

@graph.name: The church jumps over its own weathercock
@graph.source_quote: so that the church jumped over its own weathercock

@graph.name: He holds Pip by the arms
@graph.source_quote: Then, he held me by the arms

@graph.name: Pip is in an upright position on the top of the stone
@graph.source_quote: in an upright position on the top of the stone

@graph.name: Magwitch speaks in fearful terms
@graph.source_quote: went on in these fearful terms

@graph.name: Magwitch demands items from Pip
@graph.source_quote: You bring me, to-morrow morning early, that file and them wittles.

@graph.name: Magwitch threatens Pip
@graph.source_quote: You fail, or you go from my words in any partickler, no matter how small it is, and your heart and your liver shall be tore out, roasted, and ate.

@graph.name: Magwitch reveals he is not alone
@graph.source_quote: Now, I ain’t alone, as you may think I am.

@graph.name: Magwitch mentions the young man
@graph.source_quote: There’s a young man hid with me, in comparison with which young man I am a Angel.

@graph.name: Magwitch states the young man hears him
@graph.source_quote: That young man hears the words I speak.

@graph.name: The young man has a secret way of getting at a boy
@graph.source_quote: That young man has a secret way pecooliar to himself, of getting at a boy, and at his heart, and at his liver.

@graph.name: A boy attempts to hide from the young man
@graph.source_quote: It is in vain for a boy to attempt to hide himself from that young man.

@graph.name: The boy locks his door
@graph.source_quote: A boy may lock his door.

@graph.name: The boy is warm in bed
@graph.source_quote: may be warm in bed.

@graph.name: The boy tucks himself up
@graph.source_quote: may tuck himself up.

@graph.name: The boy draws the clothes over his head
@graph.source_quote: may draw the clothes over his head.

@graph.name: The boy thinks himself comfortable and safe
@graph.source_quote: may think himself comfortable and safe.

@graph.name: The young man softly creeps towards the boy
@graph.source_quote: that young man will softly creep and creep his way to him.

@graph.name: The young man tears the boy open
@graph.source_quote: and tear him open.

@graph.name: The speaker is keeping the young man from harming the boy
@graph.source_quote: I am a keeping that young man from harming of you at the present moment.

@graph.name: The speaker finds it hard to hold the young man off
@graph.source_quote: I find it wery hard to hold that young man off of your inside.

@graph.name: The speaker expresses difficulty in holding the young man off
@graph.source_quote: with great difficulty.

@graph.name: The speaker asks the boy what he says
@graph.source_quote: Now, what do you say?

@graph.name: Promise to retrieve the file
@graph.source_quote: I said that I would get him the file

@graph.name: Promise to bring broken bits of food
@graph.source_quote: I would get him what broken bits of food I could

@graph.name: Decision to visit Magwitch
@graph.source_quote: I would come to him at the Battery, early in the morning

@graph.name: Man threatens with curse
@graph.source_quote: “Say Lord strike you dead if you don’t!” said the man.

@graph.name: Pip is reminded of his undertaking
@graph.source_quote: you remember what you’ve undertook

@graph.name: Pip is reminded of the young man
@graph.source_quote: you remember that young man

@graph.name: Pip is instructed to go home
@graph.source_quote: you get home!

@graph.name: Pip expresses a wish
@graph.source_quote: I wish I was a frog. Or a eel!

@graph.name: Pip glances around
@graph.source_quote: said he, glancing about him over the cold wet flat.

@graph.name: Pip hugs his body
@graph.source_quote: he hugged his shuddering body in both his arms

@graph.name: Pip limps towards the church wall
@graph.source_quote: and limped towards the low church wall

@graph.name: Pip perceives the man picking his way
@graph.source_quote: As I saw him go, picking his way among the nettles

@graph.name: Pip imagines the man eluding the dead
@graph.source_quote: he looked in my young eyes as if he were eluding the hands of the dead people

@graph.name: Pip perceives the dead people reaching for the man
@graph.source_quote: stretching up cautiously out of their graves, to get a twist upon his ankle and pull him in

@graph.name: Pip sees the man turning
@graph.source_quote: When I saw him turning, I set my face towards home

@graph.name: Pip sets his face towards home
@graph.source_quote: I set my face towards home

@graph.name: Pip makes the best use of his legs
@graph.source_quote: and made the best use of my legs

@graph.name: Pip looks over his shoulder
@graph.source_quote: But presently I looked over my shoulder

@graph.name: Pip sees the man going towards the river
@graph.source_quote: and saw him going on again towards the river

@graph.name: The man hugs himself in both arms
@graph.source_quote: still hugging himself in both arms

@graph.name: The man picks his way among the stones
@graph.source_quote: picking his way with his sore feet among the great stones

@graph.name: Pip stops to look
@graph.source_quote: as I stopped to look after him

@graph.name: Pip perceives the marshes
@graph.source_quote: The marshes were just a long black horizontal line then

@graph.name: Pip perceives the river
@graph.source_quote: and the river was just another horizontal line

@graph.name: Pip perceives the sky
@graph.source_quote: and the sky was just a row of long angry red lines and dense black lines intermixed

@graph.name: Pip perceives the beacon
@graph.source_quote: I could faintly make out the only two black things in all the prospect that seemed to be standing upright; one of these was the beacon by which the sailors steered.

@graph.name: Pip perceives the gibbet
@graph.source_quote: the other, a gibbet, with some chains hanging to it which had once held a pirate.

@graph.name: The man limps towards the gibbet
@graph.source_quote: The man was limping on towards this latter.

@graph.name: Pip reflects on the man as a pirate
@graph.source_quote: as if he were the pirate come to life, and come down, and going back to hook himself up again.

@graph.name: Pip feels a terrible turn
@graph.source_quote: It gave me a terrible turn when I thought so;

@graph.name: Pip perceives cattle lifting their heads
@graph.source_quote: as I saw the cattle lifting their heads to gaze after him,

@graph.name: Pip wonders about the cattle's thoughts
@graph.source_quote: I wondered whether they thought so too.

@graph.name: Pip looks around for the young man
@graph.source_quote: I looked all round for the horrible young man,

@graph.name: Pip sees no signs of the young man
@graph.source_quote: and could see no signs of him.

@graph.name: Pip feels frightened again
@graph.source_quote: But now I was frightened again,

@graph.name: Pip runs home
@graph.source_quote: and ran home without stopping.

@graph.entity_name: never saw my father or my mother
